articles.author: Jon Gambrell, David Rising, Samy Magdy
articles.description: President Donald Trump has extended his deadline for Iran to reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. He said Monday that the U.S. would hold off on threatened strikes against Iranian power plants for five days. In his announcement, Trump also held out the possibility of a resolution to the war. Iranian officials denied there were negotiations. Trump’s turnaround appeared to offer something of a reprieve after rhetoric reached a fever pitch when the U.S. and Iran traded threats with potentially catastrophic repercussions for civilians across the region.
articles.title: Trump extends his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

articles.author: Lorian Belanger
articles.description: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that maritime traffic isn’t passing through the Strait of Hormuz because insurance companies are concerned about the U.S.-initiated war, not because of Iran’s actions.
articles.title: Live updates: US Central Command leader says Iran campaign is 'ahead or on plan'

articles.author: Dake Kang, Sam Mednick
articles.description: Israel’s use of Tehran street cameras to help kill Iran’s supreme leader shows how everyday surveillance can turn into a wartime weapon. On Feb. 28, Israel exploited hacked city camera feeds to track Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Hundreds of millions of cameras have been installed above shops, in homes and on street corners across the world, many connected to the internet and poorly secured. Recent advances in artificial intelligence have enabled militaries and intelligence agencies to sift through vast amounts of surveillance footage and identify targets.
articles.title: How surveillance systems are targeted by adversaries in wartime

articles.description: Authorities in New York say an Air Canada flight collided with a Port Authority vehicle after landing at LaGuardia Airport. The New York Police Department has confirmed the collision but could not immediately offer additional information. A spokesperson for the New York City Fire Department said firefighters responded to reports of a plane that crashed into a vehicle on the runway at 11:38 p.m. Additional information was not immediately available. Multiple videos taken by bystanders and posted on social media show the jet with severe damage to the front of the aircraft.
articles.title: Air Canada flight and Port Authority vehicle collide at LaGuardia

articles.author: Ryan Murphy
articles.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Air Canada flight collided with a Port Authority vehicle after landing at LaGuardia Airport late Sunday, according to authorities.
articles.title: Photos show the scene of a collision on a runway at LaGuardia Airport in New York

articles.description: In the audio recorded on Sunday night, a voice could be heard telling a vehicle multiple times to 'stop', before a regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport.
articles.title: ‘Stop, stop, stop’ heard from control tower during LaGuardia airport collision

articles.author: Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Pamela Sampson
articles.description: The U.S. Supreme Court will hear arguments at 10 a.m. ET over whether states can continue to count late-arriving mail ballots, an election issue targeted by President Donald Trump.
articles.title: Live updates: Supreme Court to hear arguments over late-arriving mail ballots

articles.description: Federal immigration officers have been seen at the Atlanta airport after President Donald Trump said he’d deploy agents to supplement the Transportation Security Administration during a government shutdown that has caused long lines at security checkpoints across the country. On Monday morning, a handful of federal agents were seen by The Associated Press near busy lines at Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport. Federal agents are a routine presence at international airports, where Customs and Border Protection officers screen arriving travelers and Homeland Security Investigations agents handle criminal cases tied to smuggling, trafficking and fraud.
articles.title: Federal immigration agents seen at Atlanta airport amid partial shutdown

articles.description: Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire. Ambulances belonging to Hatzola Northwest, a volunteer organization that provides emergency medical response, were damaged by the fire early Monday morning, according to the London fire brigade. Multiple cylinders on the vehicles exploded, causing windows to break in an adjacent block of flats, it said in a statement, adding that no injuries have been reported. Officers were called to Golders Green, a London neighborhood with a large Jewish population, after receiving reports of a fire. The cause of the fire is under investigation, authorities said.
articles.title: Vehicles belonging to Jewish ambulance service set on fire in London in apparent antisemitic attack

articles.author: Rebecca Santana
articles.description: The Senate is heading toward a vote to confirm Markwayne Mullin as the new secretary of Homeland Security. President Trump tapped the Republican senator from Oklahoma to take over the department after having fired Secretary Kristi Noem amid her handling of mass deportations and disaster response. Mullin would helm the department during a difficult time. It's in the midst of a budget battle that's leading to long lines at U.S. airports. Democrats are demanding changes in the department's immigration operations after the deaths of two Americans during protests. Mullin has backed Trump’s immigration agenda, but has tried to present a softer approach. His nomination advanced in a rare Sunday session and he is on track for confirmation Monday.
articles.title: Senate poised to confirm Mullin to Homeland Security as TSA standoff deepens
